title: EGF interviews for College Project Fanzine
display_title: EGF interviews for College Project Fanzine
wikitext:
[]: EGF interviews for College Project/Fanzine             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 11, 2013 9:14 pm Myself and some other EGF members were recently approached for an email interview; a student was working on a college project/Eva fanzine. I thought it could get an interesting discussion going, so here are my replies to the questions. SSD Interview replies  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Thu Dec 12, 2013 6:44 am Would it be of use for the student to get more replies here on the forum?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Dec 12, 2013 1:51 pm She had several people for the email interviews, and the deadline to reply to her project already past. She seemed really grateful, in any case.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Fri Dec 13, 2013 7:18 am I see. So it's more of a case of a platform for discussion and possibly partially-reluctant self-voyeurism among EGF people. But fair enough, you showed us yours, I'll show you mine. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Dec 13, 2013 12:57 pm SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Fri Dec 13, 2013 1:27 pm Chuckman wrote: I find it amusing that everyone is so in denial about the simple fact that most of what they "know" about the series and claim to be supported by official sources is just stuff they made up on their own.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Dec 13, 2013 2:43 pm Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: I was very surprised to see people being 60+ and interested in Eva and anime from as little as few years. Getting passionate about anything brand new at a late age speaks volumes about open-mindedness and curiosity.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Fri Dec 13, 2013 3:56 pm pwhodges wrote: I was fortunate enough also to choose a career (computing) in which you cannot let yourself stagnate if you want your career to continue.

I thought it could get an interesting discussion going, so here are my replies to the questions. SSD Interview replies  SPOILER: Show
